discussions.title: FastAPI & Backend Architecture
discussions.description: Production-grade REST APIs. Auth, routing, services, testing patterns in SQLAlchemy 2.0.

bullets.text: Routes → Services → Models pattern, no business logic in routes
Async sessions, relationship loading, avoiding N+1 queries
Schema design, validators, response_model patterns
Access + refresh token flow, bcrypt hashing, token blacklisting
AppException pattern, consistent HTTP error responses
MagicMock for DB sessions, exec_result() helpers, 100% service coverage
Autogenerate workflow, upgrade/downgrade safety, env.py config
Versioning, pagination, filtering patterns for enterprise clients

discussions.title: AI Tooling for Engineers
discussions.description: Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini — how I use each tool differently in my daily SE workflow.

bullets.text: Agentic coding for multi-file edits, Composer for full feature scaffolding
Architecture decisions, prompt engineering, long-context reasoning
Quick lookups, debugging rubber duck, resume and writing tasks
Research and summarization, Google ecosystem integration
Inline autocomplete, test generation, docstring writing
Chain-of-thought, role prompting, output format control
Which tool for which task, avoiding redundancy across subscriptions
Free tiers, when to upgrade, ROI per tool for job seekers

discussions.title: SaaS from Scratch
discussions.description: Group Travel OS — building a real product solo. Architecture decisions, tradeoffs, lessons.

bullets.text: Why group travel, what problem it actually solves
12 tables, relationship decisions, UUID vs integer PKs
JWT access + refresh, role-based group membership
Why services, not fat routes, for testability
Mock strategy, what to test vs what to skip
Next.js + Tailwind, why not React SPA, App Router tradeoffs
Vercel + Railway, environment variables, CI/CD basics
Connection pooling, caching layer, async endpoints

qa.a: Solutions Engineer, Technical Account Manager, and Implementation Specialist roles at US-based SaaS, enterprise software, or cloud companies. Open to remote and Chicago-based positions.

qa.q: What is your current visa/work status?
qa.a: MS CS graduate from DePaul University (2025). Currently on OPT — authorized to work full-time in the US without employer sponsorship during the OPT period.

qa.q: What makes you different from other SE candidates?
qa.a: 4+ years of enterprise implementation experience before graduate school. I've deployed to 15,000+ users, managed B2B accounts at Hilti and Daedalus, and built production FastAPI backends. Most SE candidates have one side — I have both.

A production-grade FastAPI backend I built solo. 12 PostgreSQL tables, JWT auth, expense splitting, polls, and location management. Full pytest test suite across 6 service files. Demonstrates backend architecture at enterprise scale.

qa.q: What enterprise tools have you worked with?
qa.a: ServiceNow (ticket management, post-go-live), Jira + Confluence (project tracking, SOPs), Power BI (adoption dashboards, KPI reporting), Zoho CRM (workflow re-engineering), SAP (basics), Salesforce (AI Associate certified).

qa.q: Are you available for freelance projects?
qa.a: Yes — available for FastAPI backend development, Next.js full-stack builds, and enterprise tool implementation consulting. Targeting $50–80/hr for SaaS MVPs and internal tools.
